era: 1990-1997. Punk/Grunge. E=8 -- fast, aggressive, raw, direct. Seattle. Empty Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Empty Records: Seattle punk infrastructure. First engine appearance at 291. Independent punk label distinct from Sub Pop (grunge), Hydra Head (hardcore), Suicide Squeeze (Capitol Hill indie). Documents punk wing of Seattle scene.
theory_nodes.description: Empty Records enters the engine. Seattle punk infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: Punk/Grunge hybrid at E=8. Different from post-hardcore (TAAS, 290) at same energy. Punk: short, fast, direct. Grunge inflection: heavier tones. E=8 contains both characters.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 holds; character shifts from post-hardcore to punk.

theory_nodes.concept: Return appearance: consistency documented. E=8 both visits. 1990-1997 complete arc framed. Punk commitment to approach maintained across full run. Return = confirmation of significance.
theory_nodes.description: Alcohol Funnycar's return: the punk consistency narrative.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 streak begins. Post-hardcore (TAAS) vs. punk (Alcohol Funnycar). Different lineages, same energy level. E=8 streak: 2nd consecutive degree.
E=8 streak continues. Punk/Garage. Fremont. Amphetamine Reptile. E=8: 3rd consecutive degree. Second punk return in two degrees.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=7 = E=15. Punk aggression + rocky island colonial gull screaming. Both sustained, both raw, both community-pressure acoustic environments.
